reasoning: Paul Biedermann's 200m long course freestyle record of 1:42.00 has stood since the super-suit era of 2009. The closest active swimmer, David Popovici, has a personal best of 1:42.97 and recently won the World Championships in July 2025 with a time of 1:43.53 — still roughly 1.5 seconds off the record. Furthermore, the timeframe between late October and late January primarily features short course (25m pool) competition, meaning there will be extremely limited opportunities for elite long course racing. Because no one is currently within touching distance of the record, and few long course events will be held over the next three months, it is extraordinarily unlikely that the record will be broken. In compliance with the minimum/maximum probability bounds, I am setting the probabilities at their near-maximum values, slightly adjusting downward for the longer horizon dates.
